danger_signals.visual_form: Darker, desaturated skin tones; color inversion or shift (e.g., Ice is darker than Frosty, Slyck has yellow eyes with green, purple coloring)
danger_signals.meaning: Visual corruption or moral inversion; signals antagonistic counterpart with hostile intent

danger_signals.visual_form: Angry eyes, open-mouthed frowns, glaring expressions, suspicious looks
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile disposition and threat readiness; differentiates evil twins from their good counterparts

danger_signals.visual_form: Spiked rattles, bottles hurled, farting attacks, banana saws that cut in two
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated threat level and willingness to cause harm
External threat; invasion/contamination of Mudville; signals shift from local to broader conflict

safety_signals.visual_form: Bad Mr. Frosty's reformed appearance (backwards baseball cap replacing top hat, released from prison)
safety_signals.meaning: Character has transitioned to 'side of good'; safe, trustworthy protagonist

safety_signals.visual_form: Nana Man's relaxed stance, carefree attitude; Kangoo's reluctant fighter stance
safety_signals.meaning: Non-hostile intent; characters fighting reluctantly or for benign reasons

safety_signals.visual_form: Kangoo carrying her son in battle; protective maternal visual
safety_signals.meaning: Sympathetic protagonist; fighting for stability rather than domination

safety_signals.visual_form: C-2 tournament framework; grandmaster succession structure
safety_signals.meaning: Organized, rule-based conflict; signals stability and order

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varied natural colors: white (Frosty), tan/brown (Tiny), green (Blob), yellow (Nana Man), purple-white (Octohead), pastel (Googoo), brown (Hoppy), tan (Kangoo)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Tournament badge/C-2 tournament legitimacy; rehabilitation symbolism
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Evil twins appear with darker, more saturated, or inverted color schemes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Home arenas reflect character identity (North Pole for Frosty, jungle for Nana, ocean for Octohead, toy store district for Googoo)

faction_indicators.color_identity: Darker variants: darker white for Ice, inverted/desaturated palettes, purple with green (Dr. Peelgood), purple-pale (Jack)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: None explicit; visual corruption serves as sigil
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Same base models as good twins but with altered color, stance, facial expression
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Share arenas with good counterparts; no distinct environmental territory

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Aircraft; spreading of meteor residue; declaration of grandmaster title
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Not visually present in-game until ClayFighter 63⅓
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Control of Mudville infrastructure; tournament organization as power symbol

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-meteor contamination (Mudville after clay meteor impact)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clay texture throughout environments; clay-covered city streets visible in Blob's arena; transformation of normal objects/beings into clay fighters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All characters are clay-based combatants; mutation from normal forms; loss of human/natural form

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Chaos/power vacuum (N. Boss defeated, authority contested)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tournament arena design signals temporary authority structure; multiple arenas suggest fragmented territorial control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Eight independent fighters competing for grandmaster title; no unified faction hierarchy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Inverted color palettes, darker tones, desaturated hues in evil counterparts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Evil twins fight identically but with different move mechanics; represent perversion of original character principles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Aircraft appearance; expanded meteor residue spreading; new clay generation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Tournament forced/reorganized under new authority; loss of organic competition structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Frosty's updated appearance (cap vs. top hat); prison background establishing reform narrative
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character fighting 'for the side of good' despite criminal past; demonstrates transformation possible through combat

environmental_storytelling.example: Frosty fights at North Pole (origin point), Kangoo at boxing ring (profession identity), Googoo at toy store district (juvenile/playful nature), Hoppy at military camp (Terminator spoof/weaponized persona)
Evil twins use darker, more saturated, or inverted versions of protagonist colors to signal corruption without changing model topology
Tiny's muscular frame and medicine ball attacks signal wrestler archetype; Kangoo's pouch with son signals maternal protector; Nana Man's relaxed stance signals laid-back personality
Frosty's cap replacing top hat signals character development and prison reform; Blob's expanded morphing forms signal growth/adaptation
Hoppy as Terminator spoof in military camp; Dr. Peelgood as Mötley Crüe reference; these signal pop-culture layering in Mudville's fiction

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fighting for 'side of good'; rehabilitated from incarceration; cooperative tournament participation
Boxing stance signals aggression; frowning with angered eyes; competitive superiority complex ('better than Frosty'); stole signature move
Fights despite wanting to retire; compelled by fan threats; protective maternal behavior (son in pouch); 'too nice' to opponents despite championship skill
Suspicious movement and frowning; slower, more deliberate stance; escalated violence (Banana Saw cuts in two vs. normal Banana Slice)
Terminator spoof; 'ravaging neighbor's garden'; diesel/muscular build; mop floor with opponents; excessive physical capability
Opposite of Octohead; wants to be left alone; refuses party/community engagement; moving lower lip and downward gaze signal depression or contempt; entirely different fighting style
Yellow eyes with green, yellow teeth signal sickness/corruption; heavy breathing stance; glaring with open teeth; arrogant 'number one' declaration

notes: ClayFighter 2 uses visual dualism as core narrative mechanic: good/evil twins differentiated primarily through color inversion and facial expression rather than model changes, allowing rapid visual communication of moral corruption. Arena design doubles as character biography and territorial claim. The game's world-state progression (contamination → chaos → external control) is communicated through character availability and tournament structure rather than explicit visual environmental changes. Evil twins represent perversion of principle rather than wholesale replacement, suggesting thematic interest in corruption-from-within as primary danger signal.
